profile_intro_description_1: Hello! I'm Jonghyeon Park, studying Computer Engineering now. I'm also active with the username ShapeLayer.
profile_intro_description_2: I began computer science by imitating the graduate student in CS who had the bed next to mine when I spent time in the hospital as a child. Fueled by the desire to "be great people", I dove head-first into Visual Basic, C, and Java—copying code line by line and nurturing the dream of becoming a computer engineer.
profile_intro_description_3: I got down to start programming when I was a high schooler with the development of the "Sungdeok Gallery" website commissioned by the student council, and an education software for a hands-on session at <2019 Gwangju Software Festival>.
careers_page_link: This page does not classify by occupation and subject. Please use the link provided if you received the link.

career_title_edu_bachelor_cnu_ce: Chonnam National University, Department of Computer Engineering, Bachelor's Program
career_title_edu_bachelor_cnu_jp: Chonnam National University, Department of Japanese Language and Literature, Bachelor's Program
career_title_edu_bachelor_unlv_short_ter: University of Nevada, Las Vegas as a short-term study program
career_contents_edu_bachelor_unlv_short_: I participated in the short-term training program at the University of Nevada, Las Vegas (UNLV) as a selected student from Chonnam National University's SW-Centered University Project Group, where I learned about renewable energy generation and the control, operation, and data collection of various mechanical systems used in power generation.

I toured various power generation facilities and research centers, and in particular learned how to control and collect data from solar PV and solar thermal generation equipment in Nevada's desert regions.
career_contents_edu_bachelor_exchange_sa: Excerpt from the application essay for the Mirae Asset Park Hyeon-Joo Foundation Overseas Exchange Scholarship (33rd Term):

I want to become a game developer and contribute to console game titles. In particular, I am interested in the technical aspects of how to make games that stutter even on PC run smoothly without lag on console devices with lower hardware performance than PCs. Specifically, I am currently studying a book called <Optimized C++> by Kurt Guntheroth.

However, there are limitations in learning content that is open to the public. It is not enough to work at game studios in my country that mainly develop PC games, which are relatively free from device performance and optimization issues. Therefore, I am seriously considering employment in Japan, where there are many studios producing console titles. To resolve my curiosity, it would be best to work at Nintendo, Sony (PlayStation) game studios, or game studios that continue to release game titles for these console devices.

However, I did not have the level of Japanese language ability to work locally in Japan. After participating in a conversation class where I only used Japanese to continue conversations during the winter break, I realized that I was not fluent enough in everyday Japanese before using professional terminology in Japanese.
Therefore, to expose myself to an environment where I had to live for several months and use Japanese, I went abroad as an exchange student to Japan in the coming fall semester.

career_details_project_prefix_generator: Ranked 1st in NAVER's real-time search keywords for the 10s and 20s
career_contents_project_prefix_generator: An online tool that automatically generates the '<Eom Jun-sik meme>' that was popular around 2019-2020.

During high school, I wrote it as a test while designing a final exam replacement assignment for the inter-school collaborative education program <Game Programming> course, and distributed it in web standards.
At the time, it recorded 1st place in both the 10s and 20s sections in the age-specific real-time search ranking service by Naver.

career_contents_project_zodiac_complex_b: I joined the final stage of development for the visual novel game <Zodiac Complex> and provided technical support during quality control and hotfix work.
Although I joined close to the final delivery date and did not handle a large volume of work, I provided technical support by tracking and helping fix critical bugs in major systems.

career_title_activity_gwangju_sw_festiva: 2019 Gwangju SW Experience Festival <Discord Chatbot using HangulCord> Experience Booth
career_details_activity_gwangju_sw_festi: Experience program // SW development and management
career_contents_activity_gwangju_sw_fest: Participated in 2019 Gwangju SW Experience Festival
Operated the experience program <Making Discord Chatbot with HangulCord>
At the 2019 Gwangju SW Experience Festival held in Gwangju in 2019, I planned, developed software for, and operated the experience program <Making a Discord Chatbot with HangulCord>.
Participants were able to create Discord chatbots using program scripts defined in Korean.
This experience program was planned based on the assumption that the reason ordinary people cannot do simple scripting is because "programming languages are not their native language."
The organizers gave favorable reviews for its novelty, but while operating the experience program, I was able to confirm that this assumption was wrong.

career_contents_activity_cnu_club_pimm_b: I joined and participated in PIMM, a game development club affiliated with the Department of Electronic and Computer Engineering at the College of Engineering at Chonnam National University, since admission.

In 2024, after discharge, I served as the club president and operated the club for a year. During my year as president, I worked to fix problems that had continued to be identified in the club since joining.

career_contents_certification_topcit_bod: TOPCIT (Test Of Practical Competency in IT) is a software competency assessment hosted by the Institute for Information & Communications Technology Planning & Evaluation (IITP).

It evaluates creative problem-solving and practical competency required in ICT across technical domains (software, data, system architecture, information security), business domains (IT business and ethics, TC and PM), and integrated domains (integrative capability).
Competency level score criteria (out of 1,000 points)
career_contents_certification_topcit_li_: Insufficient understanding of knowledge and skills related to technical and business domains.
Understands knowledge and skills in technical and business domains.
Can apply knowledge and skills in technical and business domains to solve tasks.
Can leverage and apply knowledge and skills in technical and business domains to solve tasks.
Can proactively solve tasks by applying and integrating knowledge and skills in technical and business domains.
